# Stationpedia Ascended Mod Implementation Guidelines (2026-01-17 20:04Z)

_**User (2026-01-17 20:08Z)**_

You are implementing an IDE + simulator + mod-runtime changes for the Stationeers mod
“Stationpedia Ascended”.

IMPORTANT:
• Do NOT claim anything is fixed unless you can point to exact code changes (files + key functions)
  AND provide concrete verification steps.
• If you cannot verify something (e.g., you cannot run the game), say so explicitly and explain
  how I should verify it in-game.
• Do NOT hallucinate fixes.

This work has TWO codebases:
A) Editor + Simulator (Electron / React tool)
B) Mod runtime (Stationeers mod code + JSON loading/rendering)

All changes below must be applied consistently across BOTH.

────────────────────────────────────────────
CORE CLARIFICATION ABOUT JSON (NON-NEGOTIABLE)
────────────────────────────────────────────

• All edits MUST ultimately be written to the mod’s JSON files.
• The mod reads JSON directly.
• HOWEVER: the user must NEVER be required to manually edit JSON as their primary workflow.
• JSON is a persistence/output format, not the authoring interface.
• All user edits occur via GUI → internal AST → serialized JSON.

────────────────────────────────────────────
CRITICAL SCHEMA REFACTOR: OPERATIONAL DETAILS
────────────────────────────────────────────

This is a schema-level refactor, not a UI tweak.

CURRENT PROBLEMS:
• “Flat Structure” breaks formatting and nested layout.
• Without Flat Structure, everything is forcibly wrapped in “Operational Details”.
• “Operational Details” is hard-coded, cannot be renamed, and behaves inconsistently.
• Nested boxes look broken and do not match in-game behavior.

THE “FLAT STRUCTURE” CONCEPT MUST BE REMOVED ENTIRELY.

NEW AUTHORITATIVE MODEL:
• “Operational Details” is NOT a special wrapper.
• It is simply the DEFAULT category created when a page is initialized.
• It behaves exactly like any other category.
• It can be renamed, deleted, reordered, or replaced.

This model applies universally:
• Devices
• Guides
• Game Mechanics
• Any Stationpedia page

────────────────────────────────────────────
NEW UNIFIED PAGE STRUCTURE (REQUIRED)
────────────────────────────────────────────

Every Stationpedia page must follow this structure:

1) Image (optional, editable)
   • Uses game PNG assets from the AssetRipper folder
   • Editable in the editor
   • Rendered in simulator and in-game using the same rules as existing pages

2) Title
   • Single authoritative title
   • Editable
   • No duplicates anywhere in editor or simulator

3) Page Description
   • Supports prepend / append description blocks (as currently supported)
   • Rich text editable

4) Categories (one or more)
   • Categories are first-class objects
   • “Operational Details” is the default initial category only
   • Categories are renameable, reorderable, addable, and deletable

────────────────────────────────────────────
CATEGORY CONTENT MODEL (CRITICAL)
────────────────────────────────────────────

Inside each category, only the following primitives are allowed:

A) Nested Section
   • Behaves like current Operational Details nesting
   • Collapsible / expandable
   • Can contain:
     – Paragraph text
     – Media blocks
     – Other nested sections

B) Inline Header Block
   • Matches vanilla guides behavior
   • Header text (H1/H2/H3)
   • Followed by paragraph blocks directly underneath
   • NOT collapsible
   • Can exist between nested sections

There is NO flat mode.

All layout is composed using these primitives.

────────────────────────────────────────────
EDITOR + SIMULATOR REQUIREMENTS
────────────────────────────────────────────

EDITOR:
• Remove “Flat Structure” button entirely.
• Add:
  – Add Category
  – Add Nested Section
  – Add Inline Header
• Word-processor-style rich text editing.
• Drag-and-drop reordering of:
  – Categories
  – Nested sections
  – Inline headers
• Resizable, dockable, pop-out panels.

SIMULATOR:
• Separate window.
• Renders pages using deterministic HTML/CSS Stationpedia Renderer.
• Fully interactive:
  – Navigation (home/back/forward/search)
  – Mode toggle (Vanilla hides Ascended overrides; Ascended shows full mod content)
• Live-updating on edits (debounced).
• Uses game assets only (no invented UI styling).

────────────────────────────────────────────
MEDIA SUPPORT (REQUIRED)
────────────────────────────────────────────

• Add support for:
  – YouTube video link blocks
  – Image blocks
• Formatting MUST exactly match the existing Solid Fuel Generator device page.
  That page is the canonical reference.
• Editor must allow adding/editing these blocks.
• Simulator must preview them correctly.

────────────────────────────────────────────
DEVICE PAGE COMPLETENESS (MANDATORY)
────────────────────────────────────────────

Currently only “Logic Types” are shown. This is incorrect.

• The editor and simulator MUST render and allow editing of ALL device page categories
  present in JSON (slots, connections, modes, IO, power, atmos, network, recipes, etc.).
• Rendering must be data-driven, not hard-coded.
• Known categories → custom UI.
• Unknown categories → generic renderer + inspector editor.
• NO category present in JSON may be hidden.

────────────────────────────────────────────
GLOBAL TOOLTIP SYSTEM
────────────────────────────────────────────

• Rename “Tooltips” button to “Global Tooltips”.
• Clicking a global tooltip MUST open an editor.
• Edits must persist and reflect in simulator and in-game.
• Device-specific tooltips remain editable on device pages.
• Global Tooltips screen is only for shared tooltips.

────────────────────────────────────────────
COMPLETE DATA COVERAGE (LOSSLESS)
────────────────────────────────────────────

• 100% of Stationpedia JSON data must be preserved.
• Unknown or unmodeled fields must round-trip unchanged.
• Provide an “All Fields / Inspector” panel:
  – Shows full JSON object
  – Auto-generated editable controls for unknown fields
  – Optional raw JSON editor (advanced fallback only)

────────────────────────────────────────────
STATIONPEDIA RENDERER VISUAL FIDELITY
────────────────────────────────────────────

• There are NO cyan links in Stationeers. Remove all cyan link styling.
• Use the SAME assets the game uses (from AssetRipper).
• Do not invent UI styling.
• Add a theme mapping file (e.g., stationpediaTheme.json) mapping UI elements → asset paths.
• Add a “Theme Debug” mode showing which asset is used for each UI element.

────────────────────────────────────────────
MOD RUNTIME FIXES (IN-GAME)
────────────────────────────────────────────

1) Guide button color must match vanilla guides (light blue).
   • Find actual color used by the game. Do not guess.

2) “Vanilla Guides” font must match “Guides” font.

3) Tooltip bug when game is paused:
   • Tooltips must still appear while paused.

4) “Stationeers Survival Manual” button:
   • Must appear ONLY on its own page.
   • Must NOT appear as a button on the Guides page.

────────────────────────────────────────────
MIGRATION REQUIREMENT
────────────────────────────────────────────

• Existing content using the old Operational Details wrapper must be migrated to:
  – A normal category named “Operational Details”
  – With all nested content preserved
• No duplicate titles.
• No broken nesting.
• No data loss.

────────────────────────────────────────────
VERIFICATION / NO-HALLUCINATION REQUIREMENT
────────────────────────────────────────────

For EACH implemented item:
1) List files changed and key functions/classes.
2) Describe how it was broken before.
3) Describe how it works now.
4) Provide steps to verify in:
   – Editor
   – Simulator
   – In-game (if applicable)

If something cannot be verified locally, say so explicitly.

Do not proceed with partial fixes.
Implement these changes consistently across editor, simulator, and mod runtime.

## Plan: Stationpedia Ascended Schema Refactor

**TL;DR:** This refactor removes the "Flat Structure" concept entirely, makes "Operational Details" a normal renameable category, implements all device page categories (not just Logic Types), fixes styling/tooltip bugs, and ensures 100% data fidelity across Editor, Simulator, and Mod Runtime.

---

**Phases (10 phases)**

### 1. **Phase 1: Schema Model Refactor**
- **Objective:** Update TypeScript and C# models to support the new unified page structure with first-class categories
- **Files/Functions to Modify/Create:**
  - src/renderer/models/StationpediaTypes.ts - Add `Category` type, remove `flatStructure`
  - mod/src/Data/Models.cs - Add `Category` class, update `GuideDescription`/`DeviceDescriptions`
- **Tests to Write:**
  - `categoryModel.test.ts` - Category type validation
  - `migrationSchema.test.ts` - Old schema → new schema conversion
- **Steps:**
  1. Write tests for new Category model structure
  2. Run tests (expect failure)
  3. Create `Category` interface with nested sections and inline headers
  4. Remove `flatStructure` property from `GuideDocument`
  5. Update `DeviceDescriptions`/`GuideDescription` to use categories array
  6. Run tests (expect pass)

### 2. **Phase 2: Migration Utility**
- **Objective:** Create migration functions to convert existing content to new category-based structure
- **Files/Functions to Modify/Create:**
  - src/renderer/services/migrationService.ts - New file for migration
  - src/renderer/services/fileService.ts - Integrate migration on load
- **Tests to Write:**
  - `migrationService.test.ts` - Migration preserves all data, handles edge cases
- **Steps:**
  1. Write tests for migration scenarios (flat structure, nested, empty)
  2. Run tests (expect failure)
  3. Implement `migrateToCategories()` function
  4. Implement `migrateOperationalDetails()` to convert wrapper to normal category
  5. Integrate migration into `loadDescriptions()`
  6. Run tests (expect pass)

### 3. **Phase 3: Editor UI - Remove Flat Structure, Add Category Controls**
- **Objective:** Remove "Flat Structure" checkbox, add category management UI
- **Files/Functions to Modify/Create:**
  - src/renderer/editor/EditorApp.tsx - Remove flat structure toggle, add category buttons
  - src/renderer/editor/components/CategoryManager.tsx - New component
  - src/renderer/editor/editorStore.ts - Category management actions
- **Tests to Write:**
  - `CategoryManager.test.tsx` - Add/remove/rename/reorder categories
- **Steps:**
  1. Write component tests for CategoryManager
  2. Run tests (expect failure)
  3. Remove `flatStructure` checkbox from guide properties panel
  4. Create `CategoryManager` component with add/rename/delete/reorder
  5. Add store actions: `addCategory`, `removeCategory`, `renameCategory`, `reorderCategories`
  6. Integrate CategoryManager into EditorApp
  7. Run tests (expect pass)

### 4. **Phase 4: Editor UI - Nested Sections and Inline Headers**
- **Objective:** Add controls for nested sections and inline header blocks within categories
- **Files/Functions to Modify/Create:**
  - src/renderer/editor/components/ContentTree.tsx - Add inline header support
  - src/renderer/editor/components/InlineHeaderBlock.tsx - New component
  - src/renderer/models/StationpediaTypes.ts - Add `InlineHeader` type
- **Tests to Write:**
  - `InlineHeaderBlock.test.tsx` - Inline header rendering and editing
  - `ContentTree.test.tsx` - Mixed nested sections and inline headers
- **Steps:**
  1. Write tests for inline header blocks
  2. Run tests (expect failure)
  3. Create `InlineHeader` type with level (H1/H2/H3) and content
  4. Create `InlineHeaderBlock` component
  5. Update `ContentTree` to render both nested sections and inline headers
  6. Add toolbar buttons: "Add Nested Section", "Add Inline Header"
  7. Run tests (expect pass)

### 5. **Phase 5: All Device Page Categories (Simulator)**
- **Objective:** Render ALL device page categories in simulator, not just Logic Types
- **Files/Functions to Modify/Create:**
  - src/renderer/simulator/components/StationpediaRenderer.tsx - Add all category renderers
  - src/renderer/simulator/components/SlotSection.tsx - New
  - src/renderer/simulator/components/ModeSection.tsx - New
  - src/renderer/simulator/components/ConnectionSection.tsx - New
  - src/renderer/simulator/components/MemorySection.tsx - New
- **Tests to Write:**
  - `deviceCategories.test.tsx` - All categories render with data
- **Steps:**
  1. Write tests for each category section component
  2. Run tests (expect failure)
  3. Create section components for slots, modes, connections, memory, versions
  4. Update `StationpediaRenderer` to render all available categories
  5. Implement data-driven rendering for unknown categories
  6. Run tests (expect pass)

### 6. **Phase 6: All Device Page Categories (Editor)**
- **Objective:** Allow editing ALL device page categories in editor
- **Files/Functions to Modify/Create:**
  - src/renderer/editor/EditorApp.tsx - Add category tabs/panels
  - src/renderer/editor/components/SlotEditor.tsx - New
  - src/renderer/editor/components/ModeEditor.tsx - New
  - src/renderer/editor/components/ConnectionEditor.tsx - New
  - src/renderer/editor/components/GenericCategoryEditor.tsx - For unknown categories
- **Tests to Write:**
  - `SlotEditor.test.tsx`, `ModeEditor.test.tsx`, etc.
- **Steps:**
  1. Write tests for each category editor
  2. Run tests (expect failure)
  3. Create editor components for known categories
  4. Create `GenericCategoryEditor` for unknown categories with auto-generated controls
  5. Add tabbed interface or expandable sections in EditorApp
  6. Run tests (expect pass)

### 7. **Phase 7: Media Support (YouTube & Images)**
- **Objective:** Add YouTube video and image block support matching Solid Fuel Generator page
- **Files/Functions to Modify/Create:**
  - src/renderer/editor/components/MediaBlock.tsx - New component
  - src/renderer/simulator/components/YouTubeEmbed.tsx - New
  - src/renderer/simulator/components/ImageBlock.tsx - New
  - mod/src/Patches/RenderPatches.cs - Add media rendering
- **Tests to Write:**
  - `MediaBlock.test.tsx` - Media block editing
  - `YouTubeEmbed.test.tsx` - YouTube URL parsing and rendering
- **Steps:**
  1. Write tests for media block components
  2. Run tests (expect failure)
  3. Create MediaBlock editor component with URL input
  4. Create YouTubeEmbed renderer (thumbnail + click-to-play)
  5. Create ImageBlock renderer using AssetRipper assets
  6. Add C# rendering for media blocks in-game
  7. Run tests (expect pass)

### 8. **Phase 8: Mod Runtime Updates**
- **Objective:** Update C# mod to handle new category structure and fix bugs
- **Files/Functions to Modify/Create:**
  - mod/src/Data/JsonGuideLoader.cs - Parse new category structure
  - mod/src/Patches/RenderPatches.cs - Render categories, fix guide button color
  - mod/src/Tooltips/SPDABaseTooltip.cs - Fix pause bug
  - mod/src/Data/Models.cs - Category model
- **Tests to Write:**
  - Manual in-game verification (no automated tests for Unity mod)
- **Steps:**
  1. Update `Models.cs` with Category class
  2. Update `JsonGuideLoader.cs` to parse categories
  3. Update `RenderPatches.cs` to render all categories
  4. Fix guide button color to match vanilla (light blue)
  5. Fix tooltip pause bug in `SPDABaseTooltip.cs`
  6. Build and test in-game

### 9. **Phase 9: Global Tooltips & Inspector Panel**
- **Objective:** Rename "Tooltips" to "Global Tooltips", add editable panel, add JSON inspector
- **Files/Functions to Modify/Create:**
  - src/renderer/editor/EditorApp.tsx - Rename button, add inspector panel
  - src/renderer/editor/components/GlobalTooltipEditor.tsx - New/enhanced
  - src/renderer/editor/components/JsonInspector.tsx - New component
- **Tests to Write:**
  - `GlobalTooltipEditor.test.tsx` - Tooltip editing persistence
  - `JsonInspector.test.tsx` - Unknown field round-trip
- **Steps:**
  1. Write tests for tooltip editor and inspector
  2. Run tests (expect failure)
  3. Rename "Tooltips" button to "Global Tooltips"
  4. Create GlobalTooltipEditor with full editing capabilities
  5. Create JsonInspector for viewing/editing all fields
  6. Ensure unknown fields round-trip unchanged
  7. Run tests (expect pass)

### 10. **Phase 10: Styling Fixes & Theme System**
- **Objective:** Fix link colors, add theme mapping file, add theme debug mode
- **Files/Functions to Modify/Create:**
  - src/renderer/simulator/simulator.css - Verify/fix link colors
  - src/renderer/simulator/stationpediaTheme.json - New theme mapping
  - src/renderer/simulator/components/ThemeDebug.tsx - New debug overlay
  - mod/src/Patches/RenderPatches.cs - Fix "Vanilla Guides" font
- **Tests to Write:**
  - `themeMapping.test.ts` - Theme file validity
- **Steps:**
  1. Write tests for theme mapping
  2. Run tests (expect failure)
  3. Create stationpediaTheme.json mapping UI elements → asset paths
  4. Verify link color is #008AE6 (not cyan)
  5. Create ThemeDebug overlay showing asset origins
  6. Fix "Vanilla Guides" font to match "Guides" in C#
  7. Fix Survival Manual button visibility
  8. Run tests (expect pass)

---

**Open Questions**
1. **Category Default Title:** Should the default category always be named "Operational Details" or should users choose the name on creation? Default: "Operational Details" / User-chosen / Empty
2. **Backward Compatibility:** Should the mod runtime support BOTH old (flat/wrapper) and new (category) JSON formats during a transition period? Yes / No
3. **YouTube Embedding:** Should YouTube videos auto-play on hover, click-to-play, or just show as a link with thumbnail? Thumbnail+click / Link only / Auto-play
4. **Theme Debug Mode:** Should this be a menu toggle, keyboard shortcut, or developer-only feature? Menu toggle / Ctrl+Shift+D / Dev build only
5. **Inspector Panel Location:** Should the JSON inspector be a separate tab, a collapsible panel, or a popup modal? Separate tab / Collapsible panel / Modal

---
